steps.title: Same change order, engineering's view
steps.explanation: This walkthrough starts where NRS-01 pivoted: the same ECO, but now walking outward through engineering's authority instead of the supply-recovery path.
steps.targetObjectLabel: ECO-NR-GOU-099 — CPP-1000 impeller clearance tolerance update
steps.expectedUserAction: Search "ECO-NR-GOU-099" (or continue directly from NRS-01 step 4 without re-searching).
steps.founderNarration: What happens when engineering changes the product after production has already started? Here's the change order at the center of it.

steps.title: See the blast radius
steps.explanation: Focus on the ECO in Universe to see everything it touches, fanned out in one view — BOMs, routing, drawings, work orders.
steps.expectedApplicationState: workspaceLens=universe; ECO-NR-GOU-099 focused with 15 relationships fanned left
steps.founderNarration: One engineering decision, fifteen downstream relationships. This is the actual blast radius, not an estimate.

steps.title: The engineering bill of materials
steps.explanation: The change flows into the impeller housing's engineering BOM.
steps.founderNarration: The engineering BOM under the new revision — the structural definition of what changed.

steps.title: The manufacturing bill of materials
steps.explanation: Engineering's definition flows into the manufacturing BOM the shop floor actually builds to.
steps.founderNarration: Same change, now in the manufacturing bill of materials — what actually gets built.

steps.title: The routing
steps.explanation: The manufacturing BOM is executed through a 10-operation routing, from receipt to shipment.
steps.founderNarration: This is the routing the shop floor follows — the change has to reach every one of these operations, not just get filed in engineering.

steps.title: The superseded drawing
steps.explanation: The prior drawing revision is explicitly retained and marked superseded — the change has a documented, reversible paper trail.
steps.targetObjectLabel: Drawing Revision — CPP-1000 casting Rev B (superseded)
steps.founderNarration: Nothing gets silently overwritten. Rev B is still here, marked superseded, linked to the ECO that replaced it.

steps.title: The shop traveler
steps.explanation: The physical work order traveler carries the change onto the shop floor for the affected lot.
steps.founderNarration: And here's where it lands on the floor — the traveler that documents the recovery work order, step by step.

steps.title: Converge: one operational company
steps.explanation: Return to the Horizon commitment to show that the engineering-change path and the supplier-recovery path were the same story all along.
steps.targetObjectLabel: Horizon LNG Partners ITEM-NR-CPP-1000 risk cell
steps.expectedUserAction: Search "Horizon" and select the commitment risk cell, then click 'Radar' to view it on the Commitment Health Radar.
steps.founderNarration: Same commitment, same casting lot, same NCR, same MRB, same recovery work order — engineering's view and supply chain's view are two doors into the same building.
